column6:
Physical credentials use W45-0 format

Wallet will use W40-2
- Wallet credential holders BID: 20,000,000 to 29,999,999
- Wallet visiter credentials BID: 500,000,000 to 599,999,999

- Wallet Enabled
- Custom MiFare Classic Key 
   (See Custom Application Notes box)
- Custom LEAF Cc key

VISA MFC App Notes:
The VISA MFC app resides in Sector 9 and the authentication key is not diversified. (Sector = 9, Diversification Disabled).
This is configured by setting the least significant 10 bits of the MFC_APP_INDEX configuration word to the value 0x009.

More Helpful Hints:
#define CT_MF_CLASSIC         3
#define CA_VISA_MFC_APP       6

SystemFeatures.CardTypesApps[CT_MF_CLASSIC][0] = CA_VISA_MFC_APP;

// VISA Sector = 9, Diversification Disabled
SystemFeatures.OpSettings[MFC_APP_INDEX] = 0x0009;

#define MFC_APP_INDEX                55
#define MFC_APP_SECTOR_MASK          0x00FF
#define MFC_KEY_DIV_MASK             BIT8
#define MFC_USE_KEY_B_MASK           BIT9

START at bit 0, read length of 10

column2:
The Lk10074_Kc1 key is the "DESFire Key" supplied by VISA.
VISA has tracked a DESFire key pair (Auth and OCPSK) for a future 
DESFire app that never got designed or implemented. So, the DESFire 
auth key has been re-assigned to Leaf Kc1. The OCPSK key given to us can be ignored since Leaf cannot use it.

The MFC key VISA supplied us can be extracted from Sequoia using 
this tool command: getkeyset visa 
This MFC key should be loaded into the BLE_MFC_KEYSET (4) reader 
keyset slot with the key offset set to KEY2_OFFSET_AES128 (byte 16).

See Mike Malone to answer any further questions.
